title: Sort a Linked List of 0s, 1s, and 2s

content.problemStatement:
You are given the head of a singly linked list consisting of nodes containing only the integers 000, 111, or 222. 

Your task is to sort the linked list such that all 000s come first, followed by all 111s, and then all 222s.

You must perform the sorting **in-place** — that is, by rearranging the links between existing nodes, not by creating any new nodes.

Function DeclarationFunction NamesortListsortListsortList – This function sorts a linked list containing only 000s, 111s, and 222s.

Parameters
headheadhead : A pointer to the head of the singly linked list.
Return Value
Returns a pointer to the **head of the sorted linked list**.
If the list is empty, return NULLNULLNULL.
Constraints
1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
0≤N≤1050 \leq N \leq 10^50≤N≤105
0≤Node.data≤20 \leq Node.data \leq 20≤Node.data≤2
Sum of N over all test cases≤105\text{Sum of } N \text{ over all test cases} \leq 10^5Sum of N over all test cases≤105

Input Format


The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.




For each test case:



The first line contains an integer NNN — the number of nodes.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the node values (000, 111, or 222).




Output Format


For each test case:



Print the sorted linked list in a single line (space-separated).
If the linked list is empty, print `-1`.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes. The second line contains N space-separated integers representing the node values (0, 1, or 2).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sorted linked list in a single line (space-separated). If the linked list is empty, print '-1'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has 7 nodes with values 2, 1, 0, 1, 2, 0, 1. After sorting, the values are 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 2, 2. The second test case has 0 nodes, so the output is -1. The third test case has 5 nodes with values 2, 2, 0, 1, 0. After sorting, the values are 0, 0, 1, 2, 2.
The test case has 5 nodes with all values 0. After sorting, the values remain the same.
The test case has 5 nodes with all values 2. After sorting, the values remain the same.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a three-pointer technique. We create three dummy nodes for zeros, ones, and twos. Then, we traverse the linked list and append each node to the corresponding dummy node. Finally, we connect the dummy nodes to form the sorted linked list.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total. The time complexity is linear because we only traverse the linked list once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the dummy nodes and pointers.

code.Python3:
import sys

class Node:
    def __init__(self, x):
        self.data = x
        self.next = None

# Function to sort the linked list of 0s, 1s, and 2s (in-place)
def sortList(head):
    if head is None or head.next is None:
        return head

    zeroHead = Node(-1)
    oneHead = Node(-1)
    twoHead = Node(-1)

    zero = zeroHead
    one = oneHead
    two = twoHead

    curr = head
    while curr:
        if curr.data == 0:
            zero.next = curr
            zero = zero.next
        elif curr.data == 1:
            one.next = curr
            one = one.next
        else:
            two.next = curr
            two = two.next
        curr = curr.next

    zero.next = oneHead.next if oneHead.next else twoHead.next
    one.next = twoHead.next
    two.next = None

    return zeroHead.next

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    head = None
    tail = None
    values = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    for x in values:
        newNode = Node(x)
        if head is None:
            head = newNode
            tail = newNode
        else:
            tail.next = newNode
            tail = newNode
    head = sortList(head)
    while head:
        print(head.data, end=" ")
        head = head.next
    print()
